role_notes: PPP GB president and former GBA-1 member. Won this seat in 2020 by a 2822-vote margin and is the verified PPP face for 2026.
Listed under MWM on the PTI Central Secretariat notification dated 11 May 2026. PTI is not on the 2026 GB ballot; PTI-allotted ticket-holders contest under partner-party symbols or as Independents.
Former GB chief minister (2015 to 2020). PML-N anchor figure in Gilgit-II per the 2026 GB Assembly election article.
2020 GBA-2 winner under PTI (margin of 4 votes). Wikipedia profile lists party as IPP for 2026.
Listed on the PTI Central Secretariat notification dated 11 May 2026. PTI is not on the 2026 GB ballot so PTI-allotted ticket-holders contest as Independents unless tagged for a partner party.

2020 GBA-3 winner under PTI. Wikipedia profile lists party as PPP for 2026. Also appears on the PTI Central Secretariat notification of 11 May 2026 as Syed Sohail Abbas; we keep the Wikipedia-verified PPP attribution.
Listed on the PTI Central Secretariat notification dated 11 May 2026. PTI is not on the 2026 GB ballot; PTI-allotted ticket-holders contest as Independents unless tagged for a partner party.
2020 GBA-5 winner as Independent. Wikipedia profile lists party as IPP for 2026.
Listed under MWM on the PTI Central Secretariat notification dated 11 May 2026. PTI is not on the 2026 GB ballot; PTI-allotted ticket-holders contest under partner-party symbols or as Independents.
Listed on the PTI Central Secretariat notification dated 11 May 2026. PTI is not on the 2026 GB ballot; PTI-allotted ticket-holders contest as Independents unless tagged for a partner party.
LA-7 Skardu-1 nominee. Son of former GB chief minister Syed Mehdi Shah (who contested this seat in 2009 won 2015 3rd 2020 3rd). The 2026 candidate is the son not the father.
2020 GBA-7 winner under PTI. Listed on the PTI Central Secretariat notification dated 11 May 2026 and contesting as Independent for 2026 because PTI is not on the GB ballot.
2020 GBA-8 winner under MWM. Wikipedia profile confirms MWM and GBA-8 for 2026. Also tagged (MWM) on the PTI Central Secretariat notification dated 11 May 2026.
source: Wikipedia (candidate profile) + PTI Central Secretariat notification (11 May 2026)
